title: Cosine/sine parametrization lies on the circle
informal_statement: The point (cx + r*cos(theta), cy + r*sin(theta)) has squared distance r^2 from the center (cx, cy), for every real angle theta. This is the standard cosine/sine parametrization of a circle of center (cx, cy) and radius r.
informal_proof_idea: Expand the squared-distance expression: (r*cos(theta))^2 + (r*sin(theta))^2 = r^2*(cos(theta)^2 + sin(theta)^2) = r^2*1 = r^2, using the Pythagorean trig identity sin^2 + cos^2 = 1 (Real.sin_sq_add_cos_sq).
formal_statement_pp: theorem circle_point_dist_eq_radius : ∀ (cx cy r θ : ℝ), (cx + r * Real.cos θ - cx) ^ 2 + (cy + r * Real.sin θ - cy) ^ 2 = r ^ 2

review.review_basis: repo self-review; kernel-verified through the tracked proof-search loop

notes: Proof authority: Lean kernel via the tracked proof-search loop (episode d3dedd8a), kernel_verified on the first Solve attempt. Fills the circle_point_dist_eq_radius gap in packets/elementary/geometry/QUEUE.md (no circle-equation packet existed before this); pairs naturally with the existing midpoint_equidist. Phrased as the cosine/sine parametrization (rather than 'point p with dist p c = r') to stay consistent with this domain's squared-distance convention (avoids Real.sqrt, matching midpoint_equidist and reflection_dist) while still being a genuine, non-trivial circle fact rather than a restatement of the defining equation.
